2026-03-01T11:35:43+02:00	fix(dispatch): resolve duplicate dispatch executions on config reload Root cause: two compounding bugs in runner.go. (1) ApplyRuntimeConfig called Start() unconditionally, spawning a new scheduler goroutine on every reload — N reloads produced N+1 concurrent loops. (2) Each tick used a fire-and-forget goroutine for flush, so even a single loop could produce overlapping flush executions. Fix: Start() now uses atomic.Bool.CompareAndSwap to become idempotent — subsequent calls while a loop is running return immediately. Flush runs synchronously inside the select loop, which is already a goroutine, so ticks are naturally serialized. No behavioral change when the service starts cold. Redis not included: the race is intra-process; a Redis lock cannot prevent goroutines within the same binary from both running; the deployment is single-node with no peers to coordinate. Deploy runbook updated to remove the manual workaround steps that existed only because the code did not enforce the invariant itself. Tests: TestReloadCycleDoesNotDuplicateSchedulers and TestFlushesDoNotOverlap both pass.
